content: Green tea catechins can stabilize blood glucose when taken with glucose, leading to a more stable blood glucose level.
Green tea catechins block glucose absorption in the intestines and cells, potentially interfering with nutrient absorption.
In China, there is no clear tradition regarding the timing of green tea consumption with meals, as various practices exist.
In Korea, traditional green tea consumption has been replaced by energy drinks containing EGCG, which are widely available.
For blood sugar control, it is recommended to take green tea with a meal to prevent a hyperglycemic spike.
To avoid interference with nutrient absorption, green tea should be spaced at least an hour away from meals.
